pending.body:
finding_summary: In `src/cli/run.rs:2180-2188`, `interrupted_handoff_cleanup_candidate_with_dirs()` now treats `FileSystem::pid_record_matches_live_process()` returning `false` as proof that the interrupted owner is gone. That is not true for legacy pid files: `src/adapters/fs.rs:795-804` also returns `false` when `process_started_at()` cannot be obtained, and that lookup depends on `/proc/stat` on Linux or `ps` output parsing on BSD/macOS (`src/adapters/fs.rs:294-318`). In those environments a still-running legacy owner can be misclassified as dead and have its interrupted-state cleanup run early.
severity: medium
affected_files: src/cli/run.rs, src/adapters/fs.rs
remediation_hint: Keep waiting when the pid record is non-authoritative and start-time comparison is unavailable; only proceed once the process is definitively gone or PID reuse is positively detected.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 2 -> Plan and Implement
